violations.text: No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Black specks of debris were observed in ice. All ice was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine must be in good repair and thoroughly cleaned and sanitized prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). No sanitizer was observed attached to dispenser. No warewashing permitted until establishment has sanitizer for 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Top cold drawer at prep unit gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2026

Ice machine panel cover was observed with black gaskets that were in poor repair. Interior of ice machine also had areas of poor repair at top of basin (black material observed frayed). Ice machine was taken out of service during time of inspection. Ice machine to be in good repair prior to use. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The following has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris: -floors under counter tops and equipment -floors in walk-in -walk-in cooler fanguards The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

A coffee cup was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Coffee cup was removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine, and walk-in cooler fans have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/17/2025

Food items was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit in the sandwich prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. The prep unit was observed not in working order, food items were observed held on ice on the top and bottom draw. The middle draw food items were not being held on ice and was observed held at 50 degrees (food items voluntary disposed of) Food items on ice were moved to walk in cooler. New Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation debris. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2024
